English: antique Meaning: Antiguo significa muy viejo y por eso valioso. Example: La mecedora antigua de mi abuela vale mucho dinero. English source: If something is antique, it is very old and rare, and therefore valuable. My grandmother’s antique rocking chair is worth a lot of money.

English: applicant Meaning: Un solicitante pide ser considerado para un trabajo o premio. Example: Muchos solicitantes llegaron a la tienda cuando se abrió la vacante. English source: An applicant is someone who writes a request to be considered for a job or prize. Lots of applicants came into the store when the job position became available.

English: artifact Meaning: Un artefacto es un objeto antiguo hecho por humanos y de interés histórico. Example: Estudiamos artefactos de un antiguo asentamiento chino. English source: An artifact is an old object made by humans that is historically interesting. We studied artifacts from an ancient Chinese settlement.

English: authentic Meaning: Algo auténtico no es falso ni una copia. Example: Comimos auténtica comida italiana durante nuestras vacaciones en Roma. English source: When something is authentic, it is not false or a copy of the original. We ate authentic Italian food on our vacation to Rome.

English: chronology Meaning: La cronología de eventos pasados es el orden en que ocurrieron. Example: Aprendimos la cronología de la Segunda Guerra Mundial en historia. English source: The chronology of a series of past events is when they happened. We learned the chronology of World War II in history class.

English: diplomat Meaning: Un diplomático es un representante de su país que trabaja con otro país. Example: El diplomático español discutió temas comerciales con funcionarios de Perú. English source: A diplomat is a representative of a country who works with another country. The Spanish diplomat discussed trade issues with officials in Peru.

English: epic Meaning: Un texto épico es un poema o película larga sobre grandes hechos. Example: El poeta escribió un texto épico sobre los grandes descubrimientos del pasado. English source: An epic is a long book, poem, or movie about a period of time or a great event. The poet wrote an epic about the great discoveries of the past thousand years.

English: excerpt Meaning: Un extracto es un fragmento corto de un texto o música. Example: No escuché toda la sinfonía, pero sí un extracto. English source: An excerpt is a short piece of writing or music taken from a larger piece. I didn’t listen to the entire symphony online, but I did play an excerpt.

English: fossil Meaning: Un fósil es el resto endurecido de un animal o planta prehistórica. Example: El experto ordenó fósiles para reconstruir el esqueleto del dinosaurio. English source: A fossil is the hard remains of a prehistoric animal or plant. The expert arranged the fossils to build the skeleton of the dinosaur.

English: humiliate Meaning: Humillar a alguien significa hacerlo sentir avergonzado. Example: Me sentí humillado cuando me tropecé frente a toda la escuela. English source: To humiliate someone means to make them feel ashamed and embarrassed. I was humiliated when I tripped and fell down in front of the whole school.

English: lyric Meaning: Lírico describe un poema con mucha emoción. Example: Me gusta leer y crear mis propios poemas líricos. English source: When a poem is considered lyric, it expresses a lot of emotion. I enjoy reading and creating my own lyric poetry.

English: majesty Meaning: Majestad es grandeza o autoridad suprema. Example: La majestuosidad del castillo antiguo sorprendió a los turistas. English source: Majesty is supreme greatness or authority. The majesty of the old castle amazed the tourists who came to see it.

English: monarch Meaning: Un monarca es quien gobierna un país por herencia. Example: El monarca vivía en un hermoso palacio con una gran puerta. English source: The monarch of a country is someone who rules a country through hereditary right. The monarch lived in a beautiful palace with a grand gate.

English: precede Meaning: Preceder algo significa venir antes. Example: El huracán fue precedido por un momento de calma y cielo despejado. English source: To precede something means to come before it. The hurricane was preceded by a moment of still wind and clear sky.

English: punctual Meaning: Puntual significa hacer o llegar a la hora correcta. Example: Mi mamá odia llegar tarde; es muy puntual. English source: When someone is punctual, they do something or arrive at the right time. My mother hates being late. She is the most punctual person I know.

English: recruit Meaning: Reclutar personas significa elegirlas para unirse a una organización. Example: Reclutamos a alguien que sería el nuevo gerente. English source: To recruit people means to select them to join or work for an organization. We successfully recruited someone to be the new manager.

English: refund Meaning: Un reembolso es dinero que se devuelve al devolver un producto. Example: Pedí un reembolso porque los zapatos eran demasiado estrechos. English source: A refund is money given back to a person when an item is returned to a store. I asked for a refund because the shoes I bought were too tight.

English: register Meaning: Un registro es una lista oficial de personas o cosas. Example: En una boda, hay un registro donde firman todos los invitados. English source: A register is an official list or record of people or things. At a wedding, there is register for all of the guests to sign.

English: renown Meaning: Renombre es ser muy conocido por hacer cosas buenas. Example: Michael es un cantante de gran renombre en Nueva Zelanda. English source: Renown is the quality of being well known due to having done good things. Michael is a singer of great renown in New Zealand.

English: tusk Meaning: Un colmillo es un diente largo y curvado de elefantes, jabalíes o morsas. Example: Algunas personas cazan elefantes y extraen sus colmillos para venderlos. English source: A tusk is a long, curved, pointed tooth of an elephant, boar, or walrus. Sadly, some people hunt elephants and remove their tusks to sell them.

English: coexist Meaning: Coexistir con algo significa existir en el mismo tiempo y lugar. Example: Nuestras mascotas conviven en casa con muy pocos problemas. English source: To coexist with something means to exist with it in the same time and place. Our pets coexist in our home with few or no problems.

English: conceive Meaning: Concebir algo significa imaginarlo o creerlo posible. Example: El niño no podía concebir el tamaño real de la Tierra. English source: To conceive something means to be able to imagine or believe it. The child could not conceive the actual size of the Earth.

English: dubious Meaning: Dudoso significa poco fiable o no considerado honesto. Example: La policía pensó que la descripción del hombre era dudosa. English source: When something or someone is dubious, they are not considered honest. The police thought that the man’s description of the crime was dubious.

English: ego Meaning: El ego es la idea de alguien sobre su propio valor. Example: El ego de Kelly la hacía pensar que era una superhéroe. English source: An ego is a person’s sense of their own worth. Kelly’s ego made her think that she was some kind of a superhero.

English: elastic Meaning: Elástico es un tipo de goma que se estira al tirar. Example: Ató elástico bungee a sus piernas antes de saltar de la plataforma. English source: Elastic is a rubber that stretches when it is pulled. He attached the elastic bungee to his legs before he jumped of the platform.

English: endeavor Meaning: Un esfuerzo o intento es una tentativa de hacer algo, especialmente algo nuevo u original. Example: El nuevo esfuerzo publicitario de la empresa terminó en un fracaso horrible. English source: An endeavor is an attempt to do something, especially something new, or original. The company’s new advertising endeavor ended in a horrible failure.

English: engrave Meaning: Grabar significa cortar un diseño o palabras en la superficie de algo. Example: La pareja grabó sus nombres en un árbol de pino. English source: To engrave means to cut a design, or words, into the surface of something. The couple engraved their names onto the old pine tree.

English: excavate Meaning: Excavar significa cavar y quitar tierra para buscar algo. Example: El equipo quiso excavar el sitio con la esperanza de hallar fósiles. English source: To excavate means to dig on land and remove dirt to look for something. The team wishes to excavate the site in hope of finding fossils.

English: jagged Meaning: Irregular significa tener forma o borde áspero y desigual. Example: El nadador se hirió al caer sobre las rocas irregulares. English source: When something is jagged, it has a tough, uneven shape or edge. The swimmer was hurt when he fell on the jagged rocks.

English: locale Meaning: Un lugar es un sitio pequeño o específico. Example: El spa fue el lugar perfecto para que mi madre se relajara. English source: A locale is a small area or place where something specific happens. The spa was the perfect locale for my mother to relax and enjoy her vacation. Notes: Puede entenderse también como “lugar” o “local” según contexto.

English: magnitude Meaning: La magnitud es el tamaño o importancia de algo. Example: El descubrimiento fue de gran magnitud. English source: Magnitude refers to large size. This was a discovery of great magnitude.

English: mold Meaning: Un molde es un contenedor que da forma a un objeto. Example: Usar un molde ayuda a que salgan bien. English source: A mold is a hollow container that is used to make certain shapes. Using a cupcake mold will ensure that they come out just right.

English: outright Meaning: Directo significa abierto y sin rodeos. Example: Todos sabían que la historia del chico era una mentira directa. English source: When something is outright, it is open and direct. Everyone knew that the boy’s story was an outright lie.

English: periphery Meaning: La periferia es el borde de una zona, lugar o cosa. Example: Había un patrón floral en la periferia de la papelería. English source: The periphery of an area, place, or thing is the edge of it. A beautiful floral pattern is on the periphery of the stationery.

English: plaster Meaning: El yeso es una pasta que se endurece al secarse. Example: Usó yeso para rellenar las grietas de las paredes viejas. English source: Plaster is a smooth paste that gets hard when it dries. He used plaster to fill in the cracks in the old walls.

English: shovel Meaning: La pala es una herramienta con mango largo para cavar. Example: El chico tomó su pala y limpió la nieve de la acera. English source: A shovel is a tool with a long handle that is used for digging. The boy grabbed his shovel and got all of the snow off of the sidewalk.

English: skeletal Meaning: Se usa para algo relacionado con los huesos. Example: Estudiamos el sistema esquelético en la clase de anatomía. English source: When something is skeletal, it relates to bones in the body. We studied the skeletal system in anatomy class.

English: stumble Meaning: Tropezar es moverse de forma torpe y sin planificación. Example: Ella tropezó mucho cuando estaba ebria. English source: When you stumble, you move in an awkward, unplanned way. She stumbles a lot when she is drunk.

English: thigh Meaning: El muslo es la parte superior de la pierna. Example: Se rompió gravemente el muslo. English source: Your thigh is the upper half of your leg. He suffered from a serious thigh injury.

English: vicious Meaning: Cruel es ser violento o despiadado. Example: El perro cruel intentó morder al niño. English source: When something or someone is vicious, they are violent and cruel. The vicious dog tried to bite the small child.

English: attic Meaning: Un ático es una habitación bajo el tejado de una casa. Example: El ático tenía dos ventanas. English source: An attic is a room just below a house’s roof. There were two windows in their attic.

English: chunk Meaning: Un bloque es una pieza gruesa y sólida de algo. Example: Al romper la roca, vieron que estaba llena de bloques de oro. English source: A chunk is a thick, solid piece of something. When they broke open the rock, they saw it was filled with chunks of gold.

English: civic Meaning: Cívico se relaciona con una ciudad o su gobierno. Example: La mayoría de los edificios cívicos están en el centro. English source: If something is civic, then it is related to a town or city, in particular to its government. Most of the important civic buildings are located downtown.

English: descent Meaning: Un descenso es un movimiento hacia abajo. Example: Durante el descenso del transbordador, la Tierra se veía más grande. English source: A descent is a movement downwards. During the space shuttle’s descent, the Earth appeared larger and larger.

English: din Meaning: El estruendo es un ruido fuerte y desagradable. Example: El estruendo de la maquinaria hizo ese lugar horrible para trabajar. English source: A din is loud, unpleasant, and extended noise. The din of the rusty machinery made the factory a horrible place to work.

English: dissatisfy Meaning: Descontentar a alguien es no satisfacerlo suficientemente. Example: Él estaba descontento con su comida. English source: To dissatisfy someone means to fail to please them. He was dissatisfied with his meal.

English: fuss Meaning: El alboroto es conducta molesta o exagerada. Example: La niña hizo un gran alboroto porque no quería dormir. English source: A fuss is excited or annoyed behavior that is not useful in any way. The child made an awful fuss because she didn’t want to go to bed.

English: glamorous Meaning: Glamoroso es elegante y llamativo. Example: No hay nada glamoroso en donde vivimos. English source: A glamorous occasion is one full of beauty and excitement. There is nothing glamorous about where we live. It is very ordinary.

English: gourmet Meaning: Gourmet se refiere a comida más fina y cara. Example: Para probar un plato gourmet hay que ir a un restaurante caro. English source: If food is gourmet, then it is nicer and more expensive than regular food. In order to get a gourmet meal, you have to eat at an expensive restaurant.

English: hence Meaning: Por lo tanto expresa una consecuencia. Example: María olvidó la llave, por lo tanto no pudimos abrir la puerta. English source: The word hence shows that something is a result of something else. Mary forgot the key. Hence, we couldn’t open the door.

English: intrinsic Meaning: Intrínseco es lo propio de la naturaleza de algo. Example: El dinero de papel no tiene valor intrínseco. English source: If something is intrinsic, then it is related to the basic nature of that thing. Paper money has no intrinsic value. It is useful simply because society says it is.

English: kettle Meaning: La tetera es una olla de metal para calentar líquidos o cocinar. Example: La sopa se cocinó en una gran tetera. English source: A kettle is a large metal pot used for boiling liquids or cooking food. The soup was being cooked in a large kettle.

English: ministry Meaning: Un ministerio es un departamento del gobierno. Example: El ministerio de Comercio se encarga de impuestos de importación y exportación. English source: A ministry is a government department. The Ministry of Trade is responsible for taxing imports and exports.

English: ordeal Meaning: Una ordeal es una experiencia muy difícil o dolorosa. Example: Conducir en la tormenta de nieve fue una auténtica prueba. English source: An ordeal is a bad experience. Driving in the snowstorm was an ordeal she’d never forget.

English: outspoken Meaning: Franco significa expresar abiertamente lo que se piensa. Example: Ella fue franca al criticar las nuevas leyes. English source: If someone is outspoken, then they are not afraid to say what they think. She was an outspoken critic about the new banking laws.

English: overwork Meaning: Sobretrabajar significa exigir demasiado a alguien con trabajo. Example: Tras tres semanas con un día de descanso, Judy estaba sobrecargada. English source: To overwork someone means to make them tired with too much work. After working for three weeks with only one day of rest, Judy was overworked.

English: particular Meaning: Particular significa un elemento específico de un grupo. Example: La tienda no tenía la prenda particular que buscaba. English source: If something is particular, then it is a single, important part of a group of things. The dress shop didn’t have the particular dress she was looking for.

English: snore Meaning: Roncar es hacer ruido al respirar al dormir. Example: No podía dormir porque mi esposo roncaba. English source: To snore means to make a loud noise each time a sleeping person breathes. It was impossible to get to sleep because my husband snored.

English: soundly Meaning: Sólidamente significa de manera completa y clara. Example: Ganó la carrera sólidamente, con mucha ventaja. English source: If something is done soundly, then it is done in the best or most complete way. The nearest opponent was ten meters behind. She won the race soundly.

English: superintendent Meaning: Un superintendente dirige un departamento o edificio. Example: El superintendente cerró las escuelas por el clima. English source: A superintendent is a person who runs a certain department or building. The superintendent decided to close the schools because of the weather.

English: congested Meaning: Congestionado significa lleno o bloqueado. Example: No llegó a casa hasta tarde porque la carretera estaba congestionada. English source: If something is congested, it is full or blocked. Tom didn’t get home until after dark because the road was so congested.

English: courier Meaning: El mensajero lleva y entrega cartas o paquetes. Example: Antes de los trenes, la mayoría de mensajeros usaba caballos. English source: A courier is someone who takes and delivers mail or packages. Before trains, most couriers used horses to travel.

English: deform Meaning: Deformar es cambiar la forma correcta de algo. Example: La computadora deformó la imagen del edificio. English source: To deform something means to change it from its correct or original shape. The computer program deformed the building’s picture into an unreal sight.

English: etiquette Meaning: La etiqueta es el conjunto de reglas de cortesía. Example: En Japón, la reverencia es una forma de etiqueta. English source: Etiquette is the group of rules about how to be polite. When in Asian countries, bowing is a form of etiquette.

English: exclusive Meaning: Exclusivo significa caro y para pocos privilegiados. Example: El campo de golf era tan exclusivo que casi nadie lo conocía. English source: If something is exclusive, it is expensive and only for rich people. The golf course was so exclusive that most people hadn’t even heard of it.

English: freight Meaning: La carga es el conjunto de mercancías transportadas. Example: Los barcos solo llevaban carga valiosa como seda y especias. English source: Freight is a set of items carried on a train, boat, or airplane. Trade ships only carried valuable freight like silk and spices.

English: garment Meaning: Una prenda es una pieza de ropa. Example: El empresario limpió todas sus prendas antes de la reunión. English source: A garment is a piece of clothing. The businessman had all of his garments cleaned before the important meeting.

English: insomnia Meaning: Insomnio es la dificultad para dormir. Example: El insomnio de Nate no le permitió descansar lo suficiente. English source: Insomnia is a condition in which a person has difficulty sleeping. Nate’s insomnia prevented him from getting enough rest.

English: intuitive Meaning: Intuitivo es saber algo sin prueba o apoyo. Example: Rhonda tenía una intuición de que Shane no iría a la escuela. English source: Intuitive is knowing about something without having support or proof. Rhonda had an intuitive feeling that Shane wasn’t coming to school today.

English: liable Meaning: Propenso significa probable que ocurra. Example: En verano, los excursionistas son propensos a ver ciervos y alces. English source: If something is liable to happen, it is very likely that it will happen. During the summer months, hikers in the forest are liable to see deer and elk.

English: obsess Meaning: Obsesionarse es pensar continuamente en algo o alguien. Example: Después de ver Star Wars, Ike se obsesionó con ser Jedi. English source: To obsess about something means to think about it all of the time. After watching the Star Wars movies, Ike obsessed about becoming a Jedi.

English: overboard Meaning: Por la borda es terminar fuera de un barco y en el agua. Example: Tom y Gary resbalaron y cayeron por la borda. English source: When something is overboard, it is over the side of a boat and in the water. Tom and Gary slipped on the wet floor and fell overboard.

English: premium Meaning: Una prima es un pago más alto que la media. Example: Tony pagó una prima por la gasolina para que el coche anduviera mejor. English source: A premium is a payment that is higher than average. Tony paid a premium for the gas because it made his car run better.

English: privilege Meaning: Privilegio es un derecho especial de una persona o grupo. Example: Solo el mejor empleado tenía el privilegio de aparcar ahí. English source: A privilege is a special right given only to a certain person or group of people. Only the best employee had the privilege of parking in that spot.

English: propel Meaning: Impulsar es empujar para mover algo. Example: El viento impulsó la hoja por el aire y la cruzó la calle. English source: To propel something means to push or move it somewhere. The strong wind propelled the leaf through the air and across the street.

English: socialize Meaning: Socializar es pasar un buen momento con otras personas. Example: Me gusta socializar con mis compañeros después de la escuela. English source: To socialize is to have a good time with people. I like to socialize with my classmates after school.

English: suppress Meaning: Reprimir significa impedir que algo ocurra. Example: Reprimió su impulso de gritar para no ser visto. English source: To suppress something means to prevent it from happening. She suppressed her urge to scream because she didn’t want to be noticed.

English: tram Meaning: Un tranvía es un vehículo sobre vías impulsado eléctricamente. Example: Tomé el tranvía hasta Octava Avenida. English source: A tram is a vehicle like a streetcar that runs on electricity above ground. I took the tram to Eighth Avenue.

English: unsettle Meaning: Inquietar significa causar ansiedad o preocupación. Example: Las nubes oscuras inquietaron a Beth. English source: To unsettle someone means to make them anxious or worried. The dark clouds in the sky unsettled Beth.

English: warp Meaning: Deformarse es doblarse hasta quedar de forma incorrecta. Example: El calor del hogar deformó el reloj. English source: To warp means to become bent into the wrong shape. The woman put the clock above the fireplace, and the heat warped it.

English: accumulate Meaning: Acumular es reunir mucha cantidad con el tiempo. Example: El correo se acumuló en su buzón mientras estaban de vacaciones. English source: To accumulate something is to collect a lot of it over time. The mail accumulated in their mailbox while they were on vacation.

English: aerial Meaning: Aéreo se relaciona con estar en el aire o volar. Example: El fotógrafo aéreo tomó fotos desde un globo aerostático. English source: When something is aerial, it relates to being in the air or flying. The aerial photographer took pictures from the air balloon.

English: apparatus Meaning: Un aparato es un dispositivo usado para un propósito específico. Example: Los campistas tenían un aparato para conocer su ubicación exacta. English source: An apparatus is a device used for a particular purpose. The campers had an apparatus that showed them their exact location.

English: avalanche Meaning: La avalancha es una gran cantidad de nieve, hielo y rocas cayendo de una montaña. Example: La avalancha destruyó el pueblo de montaña. English source: An avalanche is a large amount of snow, ice, and rock falling off a mountain. The avalanche destroyed the mountain village.

English: consistency Meaning: La consistencia es la constancia de conducta o estado. Example: La consistencia de ella en arquería nos dio opciones de ganar. English source: Consistency is the state of always behaving in the same way. Her consistency in archery meant that our team had a chance to win.

English: discharge Meaning: Dar de alta es permitir salir de un lugar como un hospital. Example: Me dieron de alta del hospital después de tres días. English source: To discharge someone is to allow them to leave from a place, usually a hospital. I was discharged from the hospital after three days.

English: episode Meaning: Un episodio es un suceso dentro de una serie de eventos. Example: Vimos el episodio final emocionante de la serie de televisión. English source: An episode is something that happens as part of a series of events. We watched the final exciting episode of the TV series.

English: intact Meaning: Intacto significa completo y sin daños. Example: A pesar de sus 30 años, el barco de mi padre sigue intacto. English source: When something is intact, it is complete and not damaged. Despite being over 30 years old, my father’s model ship is still intact.

English: mortal Meaning: Mortal significa que algo no puede vivir para siempre. Example: Todos los seres humanos son mortales. English source: When a person is mortal, they cannot live forever. All people are mortal.

English: omen Meaning: El presagio es una señal de lo que ocurrirá en el futuro. Example: El gato negro fue visto como un presagio de mala suerte. English source: An omen is a sign of what will happen in the future. He thought that seeing a black cat in the street was an omen of bad luck.

English: overcast Meaning: Encapotado significa lleno de nubes, sin sol. Example: El cielo estaba encapotado por la mañana, pero luego se aclaró. English source: When the sky is overcast, it is full of clouds and is not sunny. The sky was overcast in the morning, but by noon it was bright again.

English: poignant Meaning: Conmovedor es aquello que causa tristeza intensa. Example: Las chicas lloraron al final de la película conmovedora. English source: When something is poignant, it causes a very strong feeling of sadness. The girls cried at the end of the poignant movie.

English: ranger Meaning: El guardabosques protege bosques o parques. Example: Peter quería ser guardabosques porque le gustaba el exterior. English source: A ranger is a person who protects forests or parks. Peter wanted to be a ranger because he liked spending time outside.

English: rubble Meaning: Los escombros son restos rotos de piedra y madera tras una destrucción. Example: Había escombros por toda la ciudad tras el terremoto. English source: Rubble is piles of broken stone and wood created after a building is destroyed. There was rubble all over the city after the earthquake.

English: seclude Meaning: Aislar es mantener a alguien lejos de los demás. Example: Ella estuvo aislada en una isla durante más de un año. English source: To seclude someone means to keep them away from other people. She was secluded on an island for over a year. Notes: Ambigua entre “aislarse” (s. pronominal) y “aislar” (transitivo).

English: sideways Meaning: De lado significa moverse hacia la parte lateral. Example: Jim se inclinó de lado para frenar y detener el snowboard. English source: If something moves sideways, then it moves to or from the side. Jim turned sideways in order to slow down and stop his snowboard.
English: sob Meaning: Sollozar es llorar con fuerza y voz alta. Example: Lloré cuando mi hija menor se casó. English source: To sob is to cry loudly. I sobbed when my youngest daughter got married.

English: sober Meaning: Sobrio significa serio y tranquilo, sin emociones extremas. Example: Tras el funeral, todo el mundo se sentía muy sobrio. English source: When something or someone is sober, they are serious and calm. After the funeral, everybody felt very sober.

English: speck Meaning: Una mancha o puntito es una cantidad mínima. Example: Apareció una mancha de sangre donde lo picó el mosquito. English source: A speck is a very small mark or amount. A speck of blood appeared where the mosquito had bit him.

English: upbringing Meaning: La crianza es la forma en que uno aprende a comportarse. Example: Su crianza fue muy estricta y nunca vio televisión. English source: An upbringing is the way that someone is taught to behave by their parents. He had a strict upbringing and was never allowed to watch television.

English: bliss Meaning: La dicha es una sensación de felicidad completa. Example: Cada vez que ve a su novia, siente mucha dicha. English source: Bliss is a state of complete happiness. Every time he sees his girlfriend, he feels a sense of bliss.

English: butler Meaning: El mayordomo es el sirviente principal en una casa rica. Example: Cada vez que necesitaba algo, sonaba una campana y venía el mayordomo. English source: A butler is the most important male servant in a wealthy house. Whenever he needed something, he rang a bell, and the butler appeared.

English: cramp Meaning: El calambre es un dolor fuerte por esfuerzo muscular. Example: Después del maratón, Jenny tuvo un calambre terrible en la pantorrilla. English source: A cramp is a strong pain caused by a muscle after a lot of physical use. After the marathon, Jenny got a terrible cramp in her calf muscle.

English: decorate Meaning: Decorar es hacer un lugar más bonito con objetos. Example: ¿Ya decoraste la habitación para Navidad? English source: When you decorate a room, you make it more attractive by adding beautiful things to it. Have you decorated the room for Christmas yet?

English: dilapidated Meaning: Deteriorado significa viejo y en mal estado. Example: La pintura se pelaba del edificio deteriorado. English source: When a building is dilapidated, it is old and in bad condition. Paint peeled off of the old dilapidated apartment building.

English: evoke Meaning: Evocar es hacer que surja un recuerdo o emoción. Example: La foto evocó recuerdos de su infancia. English source: To evoke a memory or emotion means to make it occur. The picture evoked memories of when she was a young girl.

English: farewell Meaning: La despedida es un adiós o la forma de decirlo. Example: Dijo adiós con una despedida antes de subir al avión. English source: A farewell is an instance of saying goodbye or a way to say it. She got on the plane after we said our farewells.

English: faucet Meaning: El grifo controla el flujo de agua o gas. Example: Cierra el grifo cuando termines de cepillarte. English source: A faucet is a device that controls the flow of a liquid or gas. Turn off the faucet when you are done brushing your teeth.

English: filth Meaning: La suciedad son cosas sucias que repugnan. Example: Había mucha suciedad y basura en la orilla del río. English source: Filth is dirt or dirty things that disgust you. There was tons of filth and trash on the shore of the river.

English: flaw Meaning: Un defecto es un error que reduce la eficacia de algo. Example: Encontramos un defecto importante en la cadena de metal. English source: A flaw is a mistake in something that causes it to be less effective or correct. We discovered a major flaw in the metal chain.

English: grin Meaning: Sonreír significa mostrar una sonrisa amplia. Example: Ese chiste me hace sonreír cada vez que lo oigo. English source: To grin means to smile broadly. That joke makes me grin every time I hear it.

English: housekeeping Meaning: La limpieza del hogar es el mantenimiento de una casa o hotel. Example: La limpieza del hogar no es divertida, pero hay que hacerla. English source: Housekeeping is the maintenance of a house or an establishment like a hotel. Housekeeping is not much fun, but it has to be done.

English: mound Meaning: Un montículo es una gran pila de algo. Example: Había un montículo de ropa en el piso de la habitación. English source: A mound of something is a large pile of it. There was a mound of clothes on the teenager’s messy floor.

English: numb Meaning: Entumecido significa sin sensibilidad. Example: Tras el agua helada, mis dedos quedaron entumecidos. English source: When a body part is numb, it does not have any feeling. After holding my hand under the icy water, my fingers were numb.

English: reckless Meaning: Imprudente significa actuar de forma peligrosa e insegura. Example: El conductor imprudente ponía en riesgo a todos. English source: When people are reckless, they act in an unsafe way. The reckless driver posed a threat to everyone else on the road.

English: slate Meaning: La pizarra es una roca oscura usada en capas. Example: El techo de la iglesia está hecho de pizarra. English source: Slate is a dark grey rock that can easily be split into layers. The roof of the church is made of slate.

English: stool Meaning: El taburete es un asiento sin respaldo ni apoyabrazos. Example: No me gusta sentarme en esos taburetes. English source: A stool is a seat with legs but no support for a person’s arms and back. I don’t find stools very comfortable to sit on.

English: testament Meaning: Un testimonio es prueba de que algo existe o es verdadero. Example: La actuación fue un testamento de su talento natural. English source: A testament to something shows that it exists or is true. The beautiful performance was a testament to the singer’s natural talents.

English: timber Meaning: La madera es el material obtenido de los árboles para construir. Example: Los árboles de está zona se cultivan para madera. English source: Timber is wood that is used for building houses and making furniture. Trees in this area are grown specifically to be used for timber.

English: valve Meaning: La válvula controla el paso de un fluido en una tubería. Example: El mecánico limpió la válvula del motor. English source: A valve is a device attached to a pipe and controls the flow of liquid or air. The mechanic removed the dirt to clear the engine valve.

English: camouflage Meaning: El camuflaje sirve para ocultar personas y objetos. Example: El camuflaje verde y marrón fue el más efectivo. English source: Camouflage is something used to hide people and things. The green and brown camouflage was best used for hiding in forests and jungles.

English: confront Meaning: Enfrentar significa tratar un problema cara a cara. Example: La confronté al salir de la reunión. English source: When you confront someone, you meet them face to face to deal with a problem. I confronted him as he left the meeting and told him I thought he was wrong.

English: contemplate Meaning: Contemplar es pensar detenidamente sobre algo. Example: Tomó un momento para contemplar el problema. English source: To contemplate something means to think about it. Mark took a moment to contemplate the math problem before solving it.

English: contend Meaning: Luchar significa esforzarse para superar algo. Example: Stacy tuvo que luchar con una discapacidad de aprendizaje en la escuela. English source: To contend with something means to struggle to overcome it. Stacy had to contend with a learning disability throughout high school.

English: cot Meaning: Un catre es una cama pequeña y portátil. Example: En el campamento, la cabaña tenía muchos catres. English source: A cot is a small portable bed. At the camp, the boys’ cabin was lined with cots.

English: enlist Meaning: Alistarse es unirse al ejército o a una fuerza. Example: En su último año les pidieron alistarse en el ejército. English source: To enlist means to join the military. In their final year at school, the students were asked to enlist in the military.

English: frontier Meaning: La frontera es la línea entre dos regiones o países. Example: Se construyó una cerca en la frontera donde el río doblaba. English source: A frontier is a border between two regions or countries. A fence was built along the frontier where the river curved.

English: guerilla Meaning: Un guerrillero participa en luchas militares irregulares. Example: La guerrilla usa tácticas de guerra de guerrilla. English source: A guerilla is a person who fights as part of an unofficial army. Guerilla warfare involves a lot of hit-and-run fighting.

English: handbook Meaning: Un manual es una guía con instrucciones. Example: El manual te dirá qué cables conectar al televisor. English source: A handbook is material that gives specific information or instructions. If you look at the handbook, it will tell you which wires to connect to the TV.

English: hesitant Meaning: Vacilante es ser inseguo o dudar antes de actuar. Example: Era vacilante para decir la respuesta por miedo a equivocarse. English source: If someone is hesitant, then they are not sure or are slow to act or speak. Although he knew the answer, he was hesitant to say it because he might be wrong.

English: lush Meaning: Exuberante describe vegetación abundante y viva. Example: La jungla era exuberante y llena de plantas. English source: If something is lush, then it is full of a variety of large, healthy plants. The lush jungle was filled with plants, trees, and vines.

English: outfit Meaning: Un atuendo es un conjunto de ropa para una ocasión. Example: El nuevo atuendo de Kelly la hacía lucir glamorosa. English source: An outfit is a set of clothes worn together, often for a certain job or event. Kelly’s new outfit made her look so glamourous.

English: paw Meaning: La pata es el pie de un animal con garras o almohadillas. Example: El gatito limpió sus patas con la lengua. English source: A paw is an animal’s foot that has claws or soft pads. The kitten cleaned its paws with its tongue.

English: splendid Meaning: Espléndido describe algo muy bueno o impresionante. Example: Su ropa se veía espléndida de la cabeza a los zapatos. English source: If something or someone is splendid, then they are very good. From his head down to his shoes, his clothes looked splendid.

English: stray Meaning: Desviarse es apartarse del camino correcto. Example: Se perdió porque se desvió del grupo. English source: To stray means to go in a wrong direction and become lost. He found himself lost because he had strayed from the tour group.

English: substantial Meaning: Sustancial significa importante o de gran valor. Example: El banco dijo que debía una cantidad sustancial. English source: If something is substantial, then it is of great importance, size, or value. The bank said that he owed it a substantial amount of money.

English: torch Meaning: La antorcha es un palo con fuego para dar luz. Example: Tomó una rama y la encendió para hacer una antorcha. English source: A torch is a stick with one end on fire that can be carried in order to give light. He grabbed a piece of wood and stuck it in the fire in order to make a torch.

English: tract Meaning: La extensión es una gran área de terreno. Example: Había una extensa extensión de bosque. English source: A tract is a large area of land. On the other side of the mountains, there was a long tract of forest.

English: vigil Meaning: La vigilia es quedarse despierto vigilando. Example: Estuvo en vigilia frente a la tumba durante tres días. English source: A vigil is a period of watchful attention at night for a specific purpose. He had a vigil in front of the tomb for three days.

English: weary Meaning: Cansado es sentirse exhausto. Example: Jane estaba cansada después de un día largo de trabajo. English source: If someone is weary, then they are tired. Jane was weary after a long day of work.

English: amid Meaning: En medio de algo significa dentro o entre ello. Example: La abeja volaba en medio de las flores del jardín. English source: If something is amid something else, then it is in the middle of it. The bee was busily flying amid the flowers in the garden.

English: backstage Meaning: Detrás del escenario ocurren los preparativos de una función. Example: Tras el espectáculo, el director fue detrás del escenario. English source: If something happens backstage, it occurs behind a theater’s stage. After the show, the director went backstage and thanked the actors.

English: billionaire Meaning: Multimillonario describe alguien con mucha riqueza. Example: La venta de sus inventos lo volvió multimillonario. English source: A billionaire is someone who has at least one billion dollars. The sale of his inventions made the inventor a billionaire.

English: brute Meaning: Bruto describe una persona violenta o tosca. Example: Mi hermano puede actuar como bruto cuando se molesta. English source: A brute is someone who behaves or looks like a violent animal. My older brother can act like a brute when he doesn’t get his way.

English: clumsy Meaning: Torpe significa con poca destreza. Example: El hombre fue torpe y dejó caer sus archivos. English source: If someone is clumsy, then they are awkward in handling things. The businessman was clumsy and dropped his work files.

English: collide Meaning: Colisionar es chocar con algo. Example: Los dos autos colisionaron por descuido. English source: To collide with something means to hit it while moving. The two cars collided with each other because their drivers were not careful.

English: culprit Meaning: El culpable es quien cometió un delito. Example: La policía buscaba al culpable del robo. English source: A culprit is someone who has committed a crime or other bad deed. The police were still searching for the culprit from the robbery.

English: evacuate Meaning: Evacuar significa retirar gente a un lugar seguro. Example: Durante la inundación, muchas familias fueron evacuadas. English source: To evacuate means to leave a place of danger to a place of safety. During the flood, many families were evacuated to higher ground.

English: flammable Meaning: Inflamable significa que puede arder. Example: La manta es muy inflamable cerca de la llama. English source: If something is flammable, then it is able to catch on fire. Be careful with that blanket near the candle. It is extremely flammable.

English: mob Meaning: La turba es una multitud grande y hostil. Example: Los trabajadores formaron una turba y destruyeron la copiadora. English source: A mob is a large crowd of people that often wants to cause violence. The copier was destroyed by a mob of angry workers.

English: premature Meaning: Prematuro significa demasiado temprano. Example: La celebración era prematura porque la bola no había caído. English source: If something is premature, then it is done too early or before the proper time. Mark’s celebration was premature because the ball hadn’t fallen in the hole.

English: resent Meaning: Resentir es sentir malos sentimientos por algo. Example: Ella resentía no haber podido tocar un instrumento. English source: To resent something means to have bad feelings about it. He resented the fact that he had never been able to play an instrument.

English: satire Meaning: La sátira usa humor para criticar. Example: Este libro es una sátira del ejército. English source: A satire is a work of art that uses humor and irony to make fun of something. This book is a satire of what life was like in the army.

English: scrutiny Meaning: El escrutinio es revisión cuidadosa. Example: Un científico debe practicar escrutinio en su trabajo. English source: Scrutiny is the careful examination of something. A scientist should always practice scrutiny with their work.

English: segregate Meaning: Segregar significa separar en grupos distintos. Example: En clase separaron a niños y niñas. English source: To segregate something means to place it in a group apart from other things. In gym class, the children were segregated into two groups: boys and girls.

English: subject Meaning: Someter es forzar a alguien a sufrir algo. Example: Los oficiales sometieron a todos a revisión. English source: To subject someone to something means to force them to do or experience it. The officers subjected everyone to a careful search before they left the plane.

English: testify Meaning: Testificar es declarar como testigo. Example: La víctima testificó sobre el robo. English source: To testify means to give evidence as a witness. The judge listened while the victim testified about the robbery.

English: tumult Meaning: Un tumulto es ruido y confusión de mucha gente. Example: No oí a mi amigo por el tumulto de invitados. English source: A tumult is a loud and confused noise made by a large crowd of people. She couldn’t hear her friend over the tumult of the other excited guests.

English: underestimate Meaning: Subestimar es pensar menos de lo que vale. Example: Perdimos porque subestimamos su nivel. English source: To underestimate something or someone means to think less of them than they really are. We lost the game because we underestimated the other team’s skill.

English: uproar Meaning: Un alboroto es un ruido fuerte colectivo. Example: Hubo alboroto cuando su equipo perdió. English source: Uproar is loud noise caused by people who are very angry or upset. The fans made a great uproar when their team lost the game.

English: accelerate Meaning: Acelerar es aumentar la velocidad. Example: Aceleró al pisar el acelerador de la moto. English source: To accelerate means to increase in speed. When he stepped on the gas pedal, the motorcycle accelerated.

English: anew Meaning: De nuevo significa otra vez. Example: Falló el examen de conducir, pero lo intentará de nuevo. English source: If you do something anew, you do it again, possibly in a different way. Although he had failed his driving test, he decided to try it anew.

English: defect Meaning: Un defecto es una falla o imperfección. Example: Todas las botellas tenían un defecto. English source: A defect is a part of something that is wrong or missing. All these bottles have a defect and must be sent back to the warehouse.

English: dreary Meaning: Desolador describe algo triste y triste. Example: La zona del bosque era desoladora. English source: If something is dreary, then it is dull, dark, and lifeless. After the fire, this section of forest is rather dreary.

English: duplicate Meaning: Duplicar es copiar. Example: Ella duplicó los movimientos de su amiga. English source: To duplicate something means to copy it. She duplicated her friend’s movements like she was in front of a mirror.

English: electromagnetic Meaning: Electromagnético se relaciona con electricidad y magnetismo. Example: Es energía electromagnética la que produce la luz. English source: If something is electromagnetic, it is related to electricity and magnetic fields. Different colors of light come from different levels of electromagnetic energy.

English: electron Meaning: El electrón es una partícula cargada negativamente. Example: La cantidad de electrones determina la carga. English source: An electron is a particle in all atoms that has a negative electric charge. The number of electrons in an atom determines its charge.

English: glide Meaning: Deslizar es moverse suavemente. Example: Con viento los pájaros pueden deslizar por el cielo. English source: To glide means to fly on extended wings with little or no effort. When the wind is blowing, birds can glide easily through the sky.

English: ingenious Meaning: Ingenioso significa muy inteligente. Example: Charles fue el único ingenioso para reparar los motores. English source: If someone is ingenious, then they are very smart. Charles was the only person ingenious enough to repair the plane’s engines.

English: innovation Meaning: Una innovación es algo nuevo y original. Example: Ella creó una innovación en el plan de negocio. English source: An innovation is a product, or an idea, that is new or very original. Mrs. Johnson made a great innovation to the company’s business plan.

English: innovative Meaning: Innovador es muy creativo. Example: Peter fue elegido líder por ser innovador. English source: If something or someone is innovative, they can think in creative ways. Since Peter was so innovative, he was chosen to lead the science team.

English: launch Meaning: Lanzar es poner en movimiento. Example: El barco fue lanzado desde el muelle. English source: To launch something means to make it go into motion. The boat launched from the dock and foated down the river.

English: meteorological Meaning: Meteorológico se refiere al tiempo y el clima. Example: La tormenta fue un gran evento meteorológico. English source: If something is meteorological, it is concerned with the science of weather. The thunderstorm was so large that it became a great meteorological event.

English: meteorology Meaning: La meteorología estudia el clima. Example: Para entender el clima estudias meteorología. English source: Meteorology is the science that studies the weather. In order to understand the weather better, you should study meteorology.

English: penetrate Meaning: Penetrar es entrar en algo. Example: El cuchillo penetró la naranja con facilidad. English source: To penetrate something means to enter into it. The knife easily penetrated the surface of the orange.

English: persistent Meaning: Persistente es no rendirse. Example: Hicieron intentos persistentes para que comiera. English source: A persistent person does not give up and keeps on working. They made persistent attempts to get him to eat.

English: propulsion Meaning: La propulsión es fuerza que empuja hacia adelante. Example: La propulsión levantó el cohete al cielo. English source: Propulsion is the force that moves something forward. The propulsion lifted the rocket into the sky.

English: simulate Meaning: Simular es copiar una acción o manera. Example: El profesor simuló el acento francés. English source: To simulate something means to copy its actions or characteristics. The French language teacher could simulate the accent of a French citizen.

English: spur Meaning: Incitar es impulsarlo a actuar. Example: El discurso del entrenador incitó al equipo. English source: To spur someone means to urge them into action. The coach’s speech spurred her team into playing the best game of their lives.

English: stimulate Meaning: Estimular es aumentar actividad. Example: La descarga eléctrica puede estimular el corazón. English source: To stimulate something means to cause or to increase activity in it. Doctors sometimes use electric shock to stimulate a patient’s heartbeat.

English: beforehand Meaning: De antemano significa con anticipación. Example: Empacó su equipaje de antemano. English source: If something is done beforehand, then it is done in advance. He packed his luggage beforehand, so he was able to leave right away.

English: blurred Meaning: Borroso es lo que no se ve claro. Example: Las fotos estaban borrosas. English source: Something blurred is not seen clearly. There was blurred photographs of what was supposed to be a flying saucer.

English: centigrade Meaning: Centígrado es la escala de temperatura igual a Celsius. Example: La temperatura subió a 26 grados centígrados. English source: If a temperature is given in centigrade, it is the same as the temperature in Celsius. During the spring time, the temperature gets as warm as 26 degrees centigrade.

English: chatter Meaning: Parlotear es hablar rápido de cosas pequeñas. Example: Los niños chateaban en voz alta. English source: To chatter means to talk quickly about unimportant things. The children chattered in the back of the classroom.

English: concerto Meaning: El concierto es música con orquesta y solista. Example: El concierto de piano tiene un solo de piano. English source: A concerto is music played with an orchestra but features a solo instrument. The piano concerto has a section in which only the piano plays.

English: condense Meaning: Condensar es hacer líquido un gas. Example: El aire frío hizo que se condensara agua en la hierba. English source: To condense a gas means to make it a liquid. The cool air made tiny drops of water condense on the tops of the grass.

English: deteriorate Meaning: Deteriorarse es empeorar con el tiempo. Example: La economía seguía deteriorándose. English source: To deteriorate means to become steadily worse. The nation’s economy continued to deteriorate despite the politicians’ efforts.

English: degree Meaning: Grado es la unidad de medida de temperatura. Example: El termómetro marcó 38 grados Celsius. English source: Degree is a unit for measuring temperature. The thermometer recorded a temperature of 38 degrees Celsius.

English: exterior Meaning: El exterior es la superficie de fuera. Example: El exterior de la nuez era duro. English source: An exterior is the outside surface of something. The exterior of the nut was hard and woody, but the inside was soft and delicious.

English: hearty Meaning: Entusiasta es alegre y enérgico. Example: La abuela terminó la historia con una carcajada entusiasta. English source: If someone or something is hearty, then they are loud and happy. The grandmother ended her story with a hearty laugh that pleased her grandchild.

English: hospitable Meaning: Hospitalario significa amable con extraños. Example: Mi padre fue muy hospitalario con mis amigos. English source: If someone is hospitable, they are friendly to strangers. At dinner, my dad was very hospitable to my friends.

English: humor Meaning: El humor puede hacerte reír o indicar el estado de ánimo. Example: Despertó de mal humor y estaba irritable. English source: Humor can be something that makes you laugh, and it can also refer to your feelings. He woke up in an ill humor, angry at everyone.

English: manor Meaning: La mansión es una casa grande con muchas habitaciones. Example: La mansión tenía más de cuarenta habitaciones. English source: A manor is a large house with many rooms. The manor had over forty rooms and beautiful gardens.

English: monastery Meaning: Un monasterio es la casa de monjes. Example: Los monjes de este monasterio son famosos por su pan. English source: A monastery is a building in which monks live. The monks at this monastery are famous for their bread and music.

English: nursery Meaning: La guardería es un cuarto o centro para niños y bebés. Example: Los niños subieron a la guardería en silencio. English source: A nursery is a room where babies and children sleep, play, or are cared for. The children kissed their parents goodnight and went upstairs to the nursery.

English: outstretched Meaning: Extendido es alargado al máximo. Example: El gato se paró con las patas delanteras extendidas. English source: If something is outstretched, then it is extended to its full length. The cat stood on its back legs with its front legs outstretched.

English: parcel Meaning: El paquete es un bulto para enviar. Example: El paquete parecía pisado al llegar. English source: A parcel is a package of things to be carried or mailed somewhere. The parcel looked like it had been stepped on by someone.

English: profile Meaning: El perfil es la silueta de un rostro. Example: El dibujo del perfil de Olivia quedó bien. English source: A profile is an outline of a face, usually as seen from the side. The drawing of Olivia’s profile came out very well.

English: winding Meaning: Sinuoso describe un camino con muchas curvas. Example: El río sinuoso dio vueltas entre las colinas. English source: If something is winding, then it follows a twisting course. The winding river turned and looped around the hills.

English: zip Meaning: Cerrar con cremallera usa el cierre de una prenda. Example: Ella cerró la cremallera de la mochila. English source: To zip something means to close it with a zipper. She zipped her backpack closed after putting her books inside it.

English: abolish Meaning: Abolir es poner fin a una práctica. Example: Lincoln abolió la esclavitud en Estados Unidos. English source: To abolish something means to put an end to it, such as a system or law. President Lincoln abolished slavery in the US.

English: amend Meaning: Enmendar es corregir o mejorar. Example: El tratado debía enmendarse. English source: To amend something means to change it to improve or make it accurate. The countries were in agreement that the treaty needed to be amended.

English: aspire Meaning: Aspirar es desear con fuerza. Example: George aspiraba a ser médico desde niño. English source: To aspire means to have a strong desire to achieve or do something. George aspired to be a doctor from a young age.

English: censor Meaning: Censurar es quitar contenido peligroso o ofensivo. Example: La ubicación de la bomba fue censurada. English source: To censor information means to remove it if it is dangerous, rude, or rebellious. To protect innocent people, the location of the bomb was censored.

English: charter Meaning: La carta constitutiva describe derechos de una organización. Example: El estatuto decía que todos pagaban impuesto. English source: A charter is a document that describes the rights of an organization or group. The company charter explained that all employees had to pay a tax.

English: constitution Meaning: La constitución es la norma suprema de un país. Example: La constitución limita a un máximo de tres mandatos. English source: A constitution is a document of principles for a government. The country’s constitution said a prime minister could only serve three terms.

English: cosmopolitan Meaning: Cosmopolita es una ciudad con muchas culturas. Example: Hay muchos restaurantes en una ciudad cosmopolita. English source: When a place is cosmopolitan, it is full of people from many different places. There are dozens of different types of restaurants in a cosmopolitan city.

English: disseminate Meaning: Diseminar significa difundir. Example: La organización difunde información sobre el tabaquismo. English source: To disseminate information or knowledge means to distribute it. The organization disseminates information about the dangers of smoking.

English: flatter Meaning: Halagar es decir cumplidos para agradar. Example: Solo me halagaba al decir que mi vestido era hermoso. English source: To fatter people means to praise them in an effort to please them. He was just flattering me when he said that my new dress looked gorgeous. Notes: El texto fuente muestra “fatter”; aquí se traduce a “halagar”.

English: infamous Meaning: Infame es conocido por algo malo. Example: Ese canal es infame por información sesgada. English source: When someone is infamous, they are well known for something bad. That news channel is infamous for presenting biased information.

English: lame Meaning: Cojo significa caminar con dificultad. Example: Tras el accidente, los heridos quedaron cojos. English source: If one is lame, they cannot walk properly due to an injury to the leg or foot. The terrible accident left many people dead and several others lame.

English: limp Meaning: Cojear es andar con dolor en pierna o pie. Example: El jugador cojeara al dejar la cancha. English source: To limp means to walk with difficulty because someone’s leg or foot is hurt. After the injury, the player limped off of the field.

English: outburst Meaning: Un estallido es una reacción súbita y fuerte. Example: Hubo un estallido de aplausos al subir el cómico. English source: An outburst is a sudden, strong expression of an emotion. There was an outburst of cheers when the comedian took the stage.

English: pathological Meaning: Patológico describe algo extremo o enfermizo. Example: El mentiroso patológico no puede decir la verdad. English source: When a behavior is pathological, it is extreme, unacceptable, and sometimes a symptom of disease. The pathological liar could not even tell the truth about unimportant matters.

English: phenomenal Meaning: Fenomenal describe algo extraordinario. Example: Su talento para el piano era fenomenal. English source: When something is phenomenal, it is unusually great. The child’s ability to play the piano is nothing short of phenomenal.

English: poll Meaning: La encuesta recoge opiniones públicas. Example: La encuesta mostró apoyo al plan antiviolencia. English source: A poll is a survey in which people give their opinions about important things. The poll showed that many people support the plan to stop gang violence.

English: remorse Meaning: El remordimiento es arrepentimiento intenso. Example: Sentí remordimiento por lo que hice. English source: Remorse is a strong feeling of sadness and regret. When I realized what I had done, I felt remorse for my actions.

English: secrecy Meaning: El secreto significa ocultar información. Example: El secreto de la organización generó nervios. English source: Secrecy is the behavior of keeping things secret. The secrecy of the big organization made the government nervous.

English: tackle Meaning: Abordar es enfrentar un problema. Example: Esas cuestiones sociales deben abordarse enseguida. English source: To tackle something means to deal with it in a determined and efficient way. Such social problems need to be tackled right away.

English: trance Meaning: El trance es un estado de desconexión. Example: La mujer llevaba a los hombres a un trance con su mirada. English source: A trance is a state where people seem asleep and have no control of themselves. The woman’s powerful eyes often put men in a trance.

English: coward Meaning: Cobarde se refiere a quien tiene miedo de arriesgarse. Example: Un bombero no puede ser un cobarde. English source: A coward is a person who lacks courage to do risky or dangerous things. A firefighter cannot be a coward. They have to be able to act quickly.

English: delete Meaning: Eliminar es borrar algo escrito. Example: Varias líneas habían sido eliminadas del discurso. English source: To delete something means to remove or erase written material. Several lines had been deleted from her speech.

English: earnest Meaning: Sincero significa honesto y auténtico. Example: La niña fue sincera al contar lo que rompió. English source: If someone is earnest, then they are honest. The child was very earnest when she told her mother how she broke the dish.

English: ethnic Meaning: Étnico se refiere a un grupo cultural. Example: La ciudad tiene comunidades étnicas. English source: If something is ethnic, then it is related to a group with a similar culture. Many sections of the city are home to different ethnic communities.

English: exclude Meaning: Excluir es no aceptar en un grupo. Example: Carol fue excluida del concurso. English source: To exclude someone means to not accept them into a group. Carol was excluded from the contest because her friend was a judge.

English: firsthand Meaning: De primera mano es de una fuente directa. Example: Para saber de primera mano, pregunta a quien estuvo allí. English source: If something is firsthand, then it is from an original source. If you want firsthand knowledge, ask someone who saw it.

English: fluent Meaning: Fluido significa hablar con soltura. Example: Ella es fluida en alemán. English source: If someone is fluent in a language, then they are able to speak it very well. She was so fluent in German that you’d have thought she was from Germany.

English: imperial Meaning: Imperial se relaciona con un imperio. Example: Estas monedas imperiales venían del Imperio romano. English source: If something is imperial, then it is related to an empire. These old imperial coins were once used in the Roman Empire.

English: inclusive Meaning: Inclusivo significa abierto a todos. Example: Un evento más inclusivo permitiría asistir a niños. English source: If something is inclusive, then it is open to all groups and people in society. A more inclusive event would have allowed children to attend.

English: legislature Meaning: La legislatura es la sección del gobierno que hace leyes. Example: El senador había servido diez años en la legislatura nacional. English source: A legislature is the section of a government that makes laws. The senator had served ten years in the national legislature.

English: linguistic Meaning: Lo lingüístico está relacionado con el lenguaje. Example: Un enfoque lingüístico para estudiar una cultura se centra en las palabras de esa cultura. English source: If something is linguistic, then it is concerned with language. A linguistic way of studying culture focuses on words within that culture.

English: monolingual Meaning: Una persona monolingüe solo habla un idioma. Example: En la economía global actual, ser monolingüe limita tus oportunidades. English source: If someone is monolingual, then they speak only one language. In today’s global economy, being monolingual limits your opportunities.

English: nationality Meaning: La nacionalidad es una identidad basada en la nación de la que vienes. Example: Su nacionalidad es alemana, pero habla francés, español y coreano. English source: Nationality is an identity based on the nation from which you come. His nationality is German, but he speaks French, Spanish, and Korean.

English: patriot Meaning: Un patriota es alguien que ama, apoya y defiende su país. Example: Cada año, jóvenes patriotas se unen a los ejércitos de sus países. English source: A patriot is someone who loves, supports, and defends their country. Every year, young patriots join their countries’ militaries.

English: prosecute Meaning: Procesar a alguien significa emprender acción legal contra él o ella. Example: A esa persona la procesaron por pescar en el río sin permiso. English source: To prosecute someone means to take legal action against them. They were prosecuted for fishing in the river without a permit.

English: racial Meaning: Lo racial está relacionado con una raza o razas. Example: Las diferencias entre los grupos raciales son de rasgos físicos y cultura. English source: If something is racial, then it is related to a race or races. The differences between racial groups are physical characteristics and culture.

English: solemn Meaning: Algo solemne es serio y serio con honestidad. Example: El profesor prefirió una relación solemne con sus alumnos. English source: If something is solemn, then it is serious and honest. The professor preferred a solemn relationship between him and his students.

English: solidarity Meaning: La solidaridad es una unión formada por responsabilidades o intereses comunes. Example: Todos los ciudadanos se unieron en solidaridad para impulsar el cambio. English source: Solidarity is a union formed from common responsibilities or interests. All the citizens came together in a show of solidarity to create change.

English: tact Meaning: El tacto es la habilidad de evitar ofender a las personas al tratar problemas. Example: Como ambos bandos no se ponían de acuerdo, hizo falta alguien con tacto para lograr la paz. English source: Tact is the ability to avoid offending people when dealing with problems. Since both sides would not agree, it required someone with tact to make peace.

English: undermine Meaning: Socavar significa traicionar o debilitar sus esfuerzos o autoridad. Example: Los documentos ayudaron a socavar la confianza de los trabajadores en la honestidad de sus jefes. English source: To undermine someone means to betray them or weaken their efforts or authority. The documents helped to undermine the workers’ trust in their bosses’ honesty.

English: constrain Meaning: Constrain significa limitar su desarrollo. Example: Jim no puede unirse a nosotros porque está limitado por planes previos. English source: To constrain something means to limit its development. Jim cannot join us because he is constrained by previous plans.

English: depot Meaning: La terminal (estación) es una terminal de autobuses o trenes. Example: Él esperó en la terminal a que llegara su madre. English source: A depot is a bus or train station. He waited for his mother to arrive at the depot. Notes: Ambigüedad: “depot” también puede significar almacén.

English: emulate Meaning: Emular a alguien significa imitarlo porque se le admira mucho. Example: Cuando era niño, siempre intentó emular a su hermano mayor. English source: To emulate people means to imitate them because they are greatly admired. As a small boy, he always tried to emulate his big brother.

English: forefinger Meaning: El dedo índice es el dedo entre el pulgar y el dedo medio. Example: Gritó “¡Ahí está!” y señaló con el dedo índice. English source: The forefinger is the finger between one’s thumb and middle finger. He shouted, “There it is!” and pointed with his forefinger.

English: guts Meaning: Las vísceras son todos los órganos internos de una persona o un animal. Example: El médico puede explicar todos los procesos que ocurren en las vísceras. English source: The guts are all the organs inside a person or animal. The doctor can tell you every process that happens in one’s guts.

English: inherent Meaning: Algo inherente es una parte natural de otra cosa. Example: El sudor es una función corporal inherente al hacer ejercicio. English source: When something is inherent, it is a natural part of something else. Sweating is an inherent bodily function when exercising.

English: intimidate Meaning: Intimidar significa asustar a otros. Example: Mi papá intimida a mis amigos cuando vienen de visita. English source: To intimidate means to frighten others. My dad intimidates my friends whenever they visit.

English: janitor Meaning: Un conserje repara y cuida un edificio. Example: El conserje de la escuela limpió la cafetería desordenada. English source: A janitor is a person who makes repairs and takes care of a building. The school janitor cleaned up the messy cafeteria.

English: moist Meaning: Algo húmedo está ligeramente mojado. Example: El suelo sigue húmedo por la lluvia de anoche. English source: When something is moist, it is slightly wet. The ground is still moist from the rain last night.

English: nope Meaning: “Nope” es una forma informal de decir “no”. Example: Me preguntó si tenía dinero y tuve que decir “No”. English source: Nope is an informal way of saying “no.” He asked if I had any money, and I had to say, “Nope.”

English: prod Meaning: Pinchar o empujar a alguien con el dedo o un objeto puntiagudo. Example: La acosadora me pinchó en el pecho con el dedo. English source: To prod means to push someone or something with a finger or pointed object. The bully prodded me in the chest with her finger.

English: ransom Meaning: El rescate es una suma de dinero pagada al secuestrador para liberar a una persona. Example: Secuestró al príncipe y exigió un millón de dólares como rescate. English source: A ransom is a sum of money paid to a kidnapper to set the person free. He kidnapped the prince and demanded $1 million as a ransom.

English: restrain Meaning: Contener a alguien o algo significa usar fuerza física para detenerlo. Example: Mike contuvo a Allen para que no llegara a la puerta. English source: To restrain someone or something means to use physical strength to stop them. Mike restrained Allen from reaching the door.

English: saliva Meaning: La saliva es el líquido acuoso de la boca que ayuda a la digestión. Example: El bebé no pudo evitar que la saliva se le saliera de la boca. English source: Saliva is the watery liquid in people’s mouths that helps in digestion. The baby could not keep the saliva from dripping out of its mouth.

English: spit Meaning: Escupir significa expulsar líquido de la boca. Example: Salió de la piscina y escupió agua de la boca. English source: To spit means to force liquid from one’s mouth. He emerged from the pool and spit water from his mouth.

English: sprint Meaning: Esprintar significa correr muy rápido durante una distancia corta. Example: Los niños esprintaron a la parada del autobús para no llegar tarde. English source: To sprint means to run very fast over a short distance. The kids didn’t want to be late to class, so they sprinted to the bus stop.

English: stunt Meaning: Un stunt o truco es algo que se hace para llamar la atención o ganar publicidad. Example: El hombre saltó sobre los autos como truco promocional. English source: A stunt is something that is done in order to get attention or publicity. The man jumped over the cars as a promotional stunt.

English: tolerant Meaning: Una persona tolerante respeta los derechos y creencias de los demás. Example: El líder tolerante pensaba que todos eran iguales sin importar la raza. English source: When people are tolerant, they are respectful of others’ rights and beliefs. The tolerant leader thought that everyone was equal regardless of race.

English: vampire Meaning: Un vampiro es un monstruo ficticio que duerme en un ataúd y chupa sangre. Example: El vampiro se acercó sigilosamente a la mujer y le mordió el cuello. English source: A vampire is a fictional monster that sleeps in a coffin and sucks people’s blood. The vampire snuck up on the woman and bit her neck.

English: yawn Meaning: Bostezar significa abrir mucho la boca e inhalar aire. Example: El niño bostezó y se estiró los brazos antes de ir a dormir. English source: To yawn means to open one’s mouth wide and breathe in air. The child yawned and stretched her arms before going to bed for the night.

English: adjoining Meaning: Adyacente significa que está al lado o unido a un edificio o habitación. Example: No pude dormir porque la gente de la habitación adyacente hacía mucho ruido. English source: If something is adjoining, it is next to or joined with a building or room. I couldn’t sleep because the people in the adjoining room were loud.

English: allege Meaning: Alegar algo es decir que es verdad sin presentar pruebas. Example: La niña alegó que su hermano mayor había escondido su muñeca favorita. English source: To allege something is to say that it is true without offering proof. The little girl had alleged that her older brother hid her favorite doll.

English: arch Meaning: Un arco es una apertura curva bajo una estructura como un puente o puerta. Example: El arco del puente no era lo suficientemente alto para que pasara el bote. English source: An arch is a curved opening formed under a structure such as a bridge or doorway. The arch of the bridge was not high enough for the tall boat to pass underneath.

English: assemble Meaning: Reunirse significa juntarse en un mismo lugar. Example: Los padres se reunieron para discutir cómo mejorar la educación de sus hijos. English source: To assemble means to get together in one place. The parents assembled to discuss ways to improve their children’s education.

English: casualty Meaning: Una víctima es una persona muerta o herida en una guerra o accidente. Example: La única víctima del accidente de auto fue una mujer que se rompió el brazo. English source: A casualty is a person killed or injured in a war or an accident. The only casualty in the car accident was a woman who broke her arm.

English: erect Meaning: Erigir algo significa construirlo. Example: El rey erigió dos torres al norte y al sur de su castillo. English source: To erect something means to build it. The king erected two towers on the north and south sides of his castle.

English: foul Meaning: Algo fétido es algo muy desagradable. Example: No dejó que su perro bebiera agua porque olía a podrido. English source: If something is foul, then it is very unpleasant. He wouldn’t let his dog drink from the water because it had a foul smell.

English: hectare Meaning: La hectárea es una medida de 10,000 metros cuadrados. Example: La granja de su familia cubría muchas hectáreas. English source: A hectare is a unit of measure equal to 10,000 square meters. His family farm covered many hectares.

English: heighten Meaning: Intensificar una emoción significa aumentar su intensidad. Example: La música agradable intensificó su disfrute de la cena. English source: To heighten an emotion means to increase the intensity of it. The pleasant music heightened their enjoyment of the wonderful dinner.

English: hospitality Meaning: La hospitalidad es un comportamiento amable y acogedor con invitados o desconocidos. Example: Los viajeros se sorprendieron por la hospitalidad del personal del hotel. English source: Hospitality is friendly behavior and entertainment, shown to guests or strangers. The travelers were amazed at the hospitality given them by the hotel’s staff.

English: mansion Meaning: Una mansión es una casa grande y lujosa. Example: La mansión tenía treinta dormitorios, dos cocinas y una piscina. English source: A mansion is a large and expensive home. The mansion had thirty bedrooms, two kitchens, and a pool.

English: outnumber Meaning: Superar en número significa tener más integrantes que otro grupo. Example: Las niñas superaban en número a los niños de la escuela por cuatro a uno. English source: To outnumber a group means to have a greater number than it. The girls outnumbered the boys at the school by four to one.

English: overjoyed Meaning: Estar eufórico significa estar extremadamente feliz. Example: Él estaba eufórico con la noticia de su ascenso. English source: If someone is overjoyed, then they are extremely happy. He was overjoyed by the news of his promotion.

English: pasture Meaning: Un pastizal es un terreno con pasto para alimentar animales. Example: Las ovejas fueron llevadas a un pastizal con más pasto. English source: A pasture is an area of land covered with grass for animals to use as food. The sheep were taken to a pasture where there was more grass.

English: petition Meaning: Una petición es una solicitud escrita dirigida a una autoridad. Example: Todos los ciudadanos firmaron una petición pidiendo al alcalde que reparara las aceras. English source: A petition is a written request asking an authority to do something. The citizens all signed a petition asking the mayor to repair the sidewalks.

English: renovate Meaning: Renovar un edificio significa repararlo o construir nuevas estructuras en él. Example: La antigua estación de bomberos fue renovada para convertirse en un edificio de apartamentos. English source: To renovate a building means to repair it, or to build new structures on it. The old fire station was renovated into an apartment building.

English: revise Meaning: Revisar algo significa cambiarlo o actualizarlo para hacerlo mejor. Example: Cuando el editor descubrió que algunos datos eran falsos, revisó el libro. English source: To revise something means to change it, or update it to make it better. When the editor discovered certain facts were wrong, he revised the book.

English: slab Meaning: Una losa es una pieza grande, gruesa y plana de piedra, hormigón, metal o madera. Example: Miré varias losas de piedra para decorar mi casa. English source: A slab is a large, thick, flat piece of stone, concrete, metal, or wood. I looked at various slabs of stone to decorate my house.

English: terrace Meaning: La terraza es un área abierta conectada a una casa o un apartamento. Example: Por la tarde, le gustaba sentarse en la terraza y revisar su correo. English source: A terrace is an open area that is connected to a house or an apartment. In the afternoons, she liked to sit on the terrace and check her email.

English: turf Meaning: El césped (turf) es una zona de hierba con su tierra de crecimiento. Example: Después del partido, el césped estaba hecho jirones. English source: Turf is a section of grass and the dirt in which it grows. After the game, the turf looked ragged.

English: analogous Meaning: Algo análogo es parecido en ciertos aspectos. Example: La relación con su profesor era análoga a la de un hijo y una madre.
